summary:
The correct answer involves using a chlorinated compound because it has been shown effective at eliminating pathogens from contaminated environments including those involving blood exposure. Chlorine-based solutions should be used when dealing with blood contamination since they provide reliable pathogen reduction compared to other agents which may lack effectiveness under real-world conditions where organic matter exists.

Blood on operating room (OR) floors is properly cleaned using chlorine-based disinfectants such as sodium hypochlorite (bleach). This choice is justified because chloride-based compounds have demonstrated high efficacy in destroying microorganisms present in blood spills, making them superior among available options for surface decontamination after biohazardous exposures.
